perturbation.original_text: For a period of three years following the execution of this agreement , the contractor shall not start up his/her own cricket related business using the customer lists, trade secrets, inventions, innovations, processes, information, records an d specificatio ns of the Disclosing Party and/or used by the Disclosing party in connection with the operation of its business including, without limitation, the Disclosing parties business and product processes, methods, accounts and procedures.
perturbation.changed_text: For a period of three years following the execution of this agreement , the Receiving Party shall not start up his/her own cricket related business using the customer lists, trade secrets, inventions, innovations, processes, information, records an d specificatio ns of the Information Provider and/or used by the Information Provider in connection with the operation of its business including, without limitation, the Information Provider business and product processes, methods, accounts and procedures.
perturbation.explanation: Here, 'Disclosing Party' is replaced with 'Information Provider,' but only in *this* specific clause. Because the rest of the document predominantly uses 'Disclosing Party,' it creates ambiguity as to whether this clause applies only if the 'Information Provider' and 'Disclosing Party' are demonstrably identical, or if it's a separate restriction altogether. The term 'Information Provider' now appears in this single sentence, while the rest of the text uses 'Disclosing Party', creating confusion.
perturbation.contradicted_text: For a period of three years following the execution of this agreement , the Receiving Party shall not start up his/her own cricket related business using the customer lists, trade secrets, inventions, innovations, processes, information, records an d specificatio ns of the Information Provider and/or used by the Information Provider in connection with the operation of its business including, without limitation, the Information Provider business and product processes, methods, accounts and procedures.
perturbation.justification: The changed text replaces 'Disclosing Party' with 'Information Provider' in a single clause. The contradicted text uses 'Information Provider'. Because the rest of the document predominantly uses 'Disclosing Party,' it creates ambiguity as to whether this clause applies only if the 'Information Provider' and 'Disclosing Party' are demonstrably identical, or if it's a separate restriction altogether. This aligns with the definition of Misaligned Terminology - In Text Contradiction, where using multiple terms interchangeably without defining them leads to conflicting obligations.

perturbation.original_text: Without granting any rig ht or license, the Disclosing Party agrees that the foregoing shall not apply with respect to any informa tion after five years following the disclosure thereof or any information that the Receiving Party can document (i) is or becomes (through no improper action or inaction by the Receiving Party or any affiliate, agent, consultant or employee) generally available to the public, or (ii) was in its possession or known by it prior to receipt from the Disclosing Party as evidenced in writing, exce pt to the extent that such information was unlawfully appropriated, or (iii) was rightfully disclosed to it by a third party, or (iv) was independently developed without use of any Proprietary Information of the Disclosing Party.
perturbation.changed_text: Without granting any rig ht or license, the Last Man Stands agrees that the foregoing shall not apply with respect to any informa tion after five years following the disclosure thereof or any information that the Data Recipient can document (i) is or becomes (through no improper action or inaction by the Data Recipient or any affiliate, agent, consultant or employee) generally available to the public, or (ii) was in its possession or known by it prior to receipt from the Last Man Stands as evidenced in writing, exce pt to the extent that such information was unlawfully appropriated, or (iii) was rightfully disclosed to it by a third party, or (iv) was independently developed without use of any Proprietary Information of the Last Man Stands.
perturbation.explanation: Replacing 'Disclosing Party' with 'Last Man Stands' and 'Receiving Party' with 'Data Recipient' inconsistently within this section, while the rest of the document uses the original terms, causes confusion about the scope of the exceptions. It's unclear if these exceptions *only* apply when 'Last Man Stands' is the discloser and 'Data Recipient' is the receiver, or if they apply generally, regardless of who is disclosing/receiving, since the defined terms from the beginning of the contract are still referenced elsewhere. The terms Last Man Stands and Data Recipient are used once, and the rest is Disclosing Party and Receiving Party.
perturbation.contradicted_text: Without granting any rig ht or license, the Last Man Stands agrees that the foregoing shall not apply with respect to any informa tion after five years following the disclosure thereof or any information that the Data Recipient can document (i) is or becomes (through no improper action or inaction by the Data Recipient or any affiliate, agent, consultant or employee) generally available to the public, or (ii) was in its possession or known by it prior to receipt from the Last Man Stands as evidenced in writing, exce pt to the extent that such information was unlawfully appropriated, or (iii) was rightfully disclosed to it by a third party, or (iv) was independently developed without use of any Proprietary Information of the Last Man Stands.
perturbation.justification: The original text uses 'Disclosing Party' and 'Receiving Party', and the Changed Text replaces these with 'Last Man Stands' and 'Data Recipient' *inconsistently*. The Contradicted Text section also uses 'Last Man Stands' and 'Data Recipient'. The explanation states that this creates confusion about the scope of exceptions because the document might still use the original terms, which would cause a contradiction. This aligns with the definition of 'Misaligned Terminology - In Text Contradiction' because the interchangeable use of terms leads to conflicting obligations.

perturbation.original_text: Immediately upon the written request by the Disclosing Party at any time, th e Receiving Party will return to the Disclosing Party all Proprietary Information and all documents or media containing any such Proprietary Information and any and all copies or extr acts thereof, save that where such Proprietary Information is a form inca pable of return or has been copied or transcribed into another document, it shall be destroyed or erased, as appropriate.
perturbation.changed_text: Immediately upon the written request by the Information Provider at any time, th e Receiving Party will return to the Disclosing Party all Proprietary Information and all documents or media containing any such Proprietary Information and any and all copies or extr acts thereof, save that where such Proprietary Information is a form inca pable of return or has been copied or transcribed into another document, it shall be destroyed or erased, as appropriate.
perturbation.explanation: Using both 'Information Provider' and 'Disclosing Party' in the same sentence introduces confusion. Does the request need to come from both, or only one? Is 'Information Provider' intended to be identical to 'Disclosing Party' in this context? The sentence now contains both 'Information Provider' and 'Disclosing Party', causing a contradiction within the obligation to return the information.
perturbation.contradicted_text: Immediately upon the written request by the Information Provider at any time, th e Receiving Party will return to the Disclosing Party all Proprietary Information and all documents or media containing any such Proprietary Information and any and all copies or extr acts thereof, save that where such Proprietary Information is a form inca pable of return or has been copied or transcribed into another document, it shall be destroyed or erased, as appropriate.
perturbation.justification: The changed text introduces both 'Information Provider' and 'Disclosing Party' within the same clause defining the return of proprietary information. This creates a contradiction because it is unclear whether a request from either party is sufficient or if both parties must request the return. The contradicted text uses both 'Information Provider' and 'Disclosing Party' creating uncertainty in which partys request matters. This ambiguity directly contradicts the intent of a clear process for requesting information return, and leads to a Misaligned Terminology contradiction

perturbation.original_text: The Receiving Party understands that nothing herein (i) requires the disclosure of any Proprietary Information or (ii) requir es the Disclosing Party to proceed with any transaction or relationship.
perturbation.changed_text: The Data Recipient understands that nothing herein (i) requires the disclosure of any Proprietary Information or (ii) requir es the Last Man Stands to proceed with any transaction or relationship.
perturbation.explanation: Replacing 'Receiving Party' with 'Data Recipient' and 'Disclosing Party' with 'Last Man Stands' creates a contradiction. Throughout the document, many sections reference 'Receiving Party' and 'Disclosing Party'. By replacing these terms with 'Data Recipient' and 'Last Man Stands' only in Section 4, the reader may now question whether that Section refers to the same parties as 'Receiving Party' and 'Disclosing Party', or whether new obligations are imposed upon 'Data Recipient' and 'Last Man Stands' in their individual capacities. Also this substitution leaves 'Receiving Party', undefined in the context of this section.
perturbation.contradicted_text: The Data Recipient understands that nothing herein (i) requires the disclosure of any Proprietary Information or (ii) requir es the Last Man Stands to proceed with any transaction or relationship.
perturbation.justification: The original text references 'Receiving Party' and 'Disclosing Party.' The changed text replaces these with 'Data Recipient' and 'Last Man Stands' in Section 4. Because other sections of the contract reference 'Receiving Party' and 'Disclosing Party' (as shown in the contradicted text which uses 'Data Recipient' and 'Last Man Stands'), this introduces ambiguity and creates a contradiction. The reader is left unsure if the obligations in Section 4 apply to the original parties or to new distinct entities, leading to a contradiction and uncertainty as described in the Misaligned Terminology definition.
